title: Pyrex 3 Piece Measuring Cup Set, Includes 1, 2, and 4 Tempered Glass Liquid Measuring Cups, Dishwasher, Freezer, Microwave, and Oven Safe, Essential Kitchen Tools
description:
About this item
3-PIECE: Includes 1-cup, 2-cup, and 4-cup measuring cups that are perfect for baking, cooking, mixing and more. Dimensions for 1 cup: 6'' diameter x 4.5'' high. Dimensions for 2 cup: 6.5'' diameter x 4.5'' high. Dimensions for 4 cup: 8'' diameter x 6'' high.
EASY TO READ: Featuring bold and large measurements in both ounces and milliliters making this effortless to read.
EASY GRIP HANDLES: Adding milk to your cake batter, We got you covered. All Pyrex measuring cups feature a curved spout for non-mess pouring and easy-grip handles for comfort.
MICROWAVABLE: Need to melt a cup of butter for a batch of chocolate cookies, Just throw our Pyrex Measuring Cup in the microwave. High-quality tempered glass is dishwasher, freezer, microwave and preheated oven safe. Non-porous glass is BPA free, non-toxic and won’t absorb food odors, flavors, or stains.
Pyrex IS THE #1 BRAND OF GLASS PREPWARE*: Loved for generations, experienced cooks and beginners alike have reached for Pyrex brand glassware products, because they’re affordable, durable, and great for all their cooking, serving and storing needs. Source: The NPD Group / Retail Tracking Service, dollars, 12 ME December 2022
reviews_5s:
I love that this set has both Imperial and Metric measures on it. I bought it for my apartment in Brazil, but my recipes (and me) are all from the US. This saves me the time and trouble of trying to calculate conversions when cooking.
These are the classic Pyrex glass measuring cups, made to last a lifetime and then some. I have a similar set I purchased for my house in the US back in 1980, and those are still in excellent condition. I know these will be well loved by someone long after I am gone...I like these glass measuring cups, they are well made, the measurements are clear and they last a long time. My entire family has been using them for over half a century. I like this set because you can cook one recipe that has different ingredients, and prepare them separately. They came well packed, and it was delivered promptly. For me they are the best measuring cups in the market. Recommend them.I bought these to replace my plastic ones I’ve had for 13 years! My mom actually recommended I get these since she has some & they’ve lasted for over 50 years! I love that you get 3 for such a great price & with the set of 3 I always have one that’s clean on hand. I’m very happy!I use this set at least daily- so nice to have the 3 different sizes. A staple for your kitchenPurchased as a gift for Xmas. She loved the different sizes. Measurements seem accurate and are easily readable with the red measurements. Good quality.The measuring cups are wider than older ones, which makes it easier to wash them. The widest cup is extremely useful. Good quality and design, a bit heavy.Sturdy, good quality, easy to useI broke our old two cup Pyrex measuring cup - had it for over 30 years. So I found this set of 3 sizes and got them - my wife thought it was dumb at first, then after about two weeks told me she has been surprised at how many times she uses 2 or all 3 when cooking - and then when baking - well uses all 3 every time. She said she thought the large measuring cup would never be used… but uses it at least on half our meals. I got lucky.Over the years my supply of Pyrex measuring cups had diminished. So I was ready to replenish my stock and I found this selection on Amazon. They are shaped a little differently and they are larger than my old school products. None of that matters and I'm just thrilled that this selection was available on Amazon at a reasonable price.I’ve had Pyrex measuring cups for a very long time. The red measuring marks on a couple have faded, so I treated my self to replacement cups, Glad I did: easy to read and the three sizes stack inside each other.
reviews_1s:
I'm extremely disappointed with my Pyrex Glass measuring cup. After less than four months of use, the measuring lines have almost completely disappeared, making it useless for accurate measurements. To make matters worse, the design of the cup makes it incredibly difficult to pour without spilling the contents.
I expected more from a reputable brand like Pyrex, known for their high-quality glass products.
Given the problems I've experienced, I would not recommend this product to others. There are likely better options available that can withstand regular use without losing their functionality.What happened to Pyrex. Not the Pyrex of old, design has changed and you should read.

Replacing older cups with worn print.

The ability to pour without spilling is gone. I brew hot tea, strain it into the large measuring cup, then pour it into mugs. Hot, hot, dripping spilled mess everywhere. No worries, I can get this right, I'll try again tomorrow. Three mornings in a row, three big wet messes and a couple of burns to sweeten the deal. I would recommend looking elsewhere if you need to pour from these cups and here is why. Pyrex simply depresses the edge of their cup to form a pouring lip. It is not formed correctly and it will drip every time, running down the side of the cup. Your fluids will rush over the edges of the cup to the left and right of this tiny pouring lip. The more you attempt to get it into your other container (tea mug), the more runs down the outside of the measuring cup! Very frustrating. In contrast, I have another design that has a hooked shaped symbol, like what would be used to anchor a boat, and it works flawlessly. Here is why. This other brand has a raised lip to the left and right of the pouring lip. This FEATURE acts as a funnel to direct the fluids (really matters when it is hot), into the pouring lip with great success.

Notice the huge difference in these Amazon pics of two different product designs. Pyrex = no thought, the other = user design form, and function features. My 2 cents. I don't do reviews, but I also do not wish for any other parents out there to find out the hard way, these can burn you OR YOUR LITTLE ONES STANDING UNDER YOU AT THE COUNTER!

I recommend Anchor Hocking 77940COM Anchor 77940 3-Piece Measuring Cup Set, Set of 3, Clear. https://www.amazon.com/Anchor-77940-3-Piece-Measuring-Cup/dp/B0015QV5S4/ref=sr_1_7?keywords=anchor+glass+measuring+cups&qid=1572961596&sr=8-7My mother has had her Pyrex mixing bowls and measuring cups for nearly 50 years. They look as good today as they did when I was a little girl. So based on that I purchased my own set. Pouring without spillage has always been an issue, but I have learned to overcome that with pouring very very slowly. The real issue is that the measurements have worn off making it just useless glass pitchers! A complete disappointment!You've seen the reviews that it doesn't pour without spilling, those are true. Same with lines wearing off. One thing that gets less mentioned is that the cups dont have both US and metric on each side. So if you are pouring from a container in your right hand into the measuring cup held by your left hand, then you will be looking at metric lines. So essentially the cup is right handed for metric users but left handed for US users. It's super annoying. You are now required to place the cup on the counter to pour anything into it.I thought I was upgrading from my old measuring cups to matching, high quality Pyrex measuring cups. But no. The first pour, simply trying to add a cup of water into the pan on the stove, dribbled all over the stove top instead of going into the pan. "Seriously?" I have learned to hold the bottom of the cup over the pan, pouring (slow or fast doesn't change the dribbling), so that it drips off the BOTTOM of the cup, not from the pour spout, to get it all into the pan. Absolute stupidity that whoever designed these didn't tweak them to fix this issue before sending them to mass production. The hunt for a measuring cup that pours from the SPOUT is still ongoing. These get a single star because the ink hasn't faded in the dishwasher, or disappeared, and the glass is heavy and not fragile feeling, plus the handles are open at the bottom so you can nest the three together in the cabinet. Just can't pour anything without it going everywhere - it's like a joke dribble cup. Joke's on me for buying them.I will echo what another purchaser wrote - the pour spout is comically small and ineffective. The area must have been deformed in creation, because there is no way to pour (even with patience) without the liquid pouring out the side and going on the counter. I get more liquid outside the bowl on the counter than in the bowl. The larger ones are slightly better, but the small single cup, I just threw away because it was useless to keep (might make a nice recycled flowerpot though.

So, what's the difference in design you might ask? If you look at older designs of the same types of cups, the pour area gently curves up to the pour lip and the lip itself is slightly wider. This new design (or at least for mine and some other folks with the same issue), there is no gentle curve up that increases the surface area before the spout lip. Mine is just this TINY little lip, so once you begin to pour, there is too much liquid for the surface area of the pour lip, so the liquid goes to the easiest point of exit...which is right out the sides, along the outside surface of the cup, and onto the counter...with just a dash exiting the lip and into the bowl where it belongs. I would not recommend getting this set, unless they fix the flow issue, but perhaps those with 5 star reviews didn't have deformed cups? Buyer beware.This measuring cup exploded when my son was taking it out of the pantry. He didn’t even clink it against anything. He didn’t pour anything hot in it. It wasnt cold in here. It didn’t just come out of the dishwasher…..nothing. He just picked it up. Luckily he only had 1 scratch. After some research I discovered that they don’t make Pyrex sold in the USA out of borosilicate anymore. It’s soda-lime. My old Pyrex never once exploded. Definitely don’t let your kids touch your Pyrex stuff because evidently this is a common problem. Maybe be research a different brand. This is so disappointing.I loved the original set of these that I purchased, but they were scratched and etched from so much use. So, when we moved I ordered a new set. Now I wish that I had kept the old set. My 4 cup and 2 cup measures have lost all of the measurement lines except the very top one. That took about a year, but that shouldn't have happened. I have not replace them because I'm afraid it will happen again. I'll be looking for a different brand soon.I bought Pyrex because of brand quality - my grandparents used and loved it, as have my parents and I. So you can imagine my surprise when I unloaded the dishwasher to find markings gone from my 2 month old measuring cup. I would expect that from a 10 year old cup, not a 2 month old cup.I was excited to get these as I've always just used my 1 cup Anchor for cooking the past 10+ years. Quickly realized these were trash when I was collecting water to boil.

The spout on these cups are terrible. You have to diligently and slowly pour for it to not spill or make a mess. I immediately swapped back to my old Anchor 1 cup soon as I noticed this with all 3 cups. I even did a side by side test with my old Anchor to make sure I wasnt crazy. Would give it a 0 star if I could.
